title: Maximum Side Length of a Square With Sum Less Than or Equal to Threshold
hints: Notice that if a square of size k has a valid sum, any smaller square inside it also does. This monotonicity suggests a specific search strategy.
Precompute a 2D prefix sum array to calculate the sum of any rectangular sub-matrix in constant time, avoiding repeated summation loops.
Combine binary search on the side length with the prefix sum array. For each candidate length, check all possible top-left positions to see if any square fits the threshold.
